post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 252
UIC Invitational V
feat. Starcraft Mafia III, BalancedTM Version



- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules for easy reference.

For experienced players: everything you need is here, and in the setup tab.
For new players: you may find it useful to familiarize yourself with the rest of the rules.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside this thread, your role PM, or approved private chats, even if you are dead. Access to spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each "Day" is 3 days while each "Night" is 1 day.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses Dawn start: Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules by completing the confirm task.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are this is an invitational so you'd better be active.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization does not matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Only code vote formats are accepted. Refer to the Voting tab if you do not know what these are.
  • Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  • Deadline hang is on from Day 1.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • Disagreements should be resolved in a way that does not affect gameplay.
  • Do not discuss rule violations by other players in the thread; contact the moderators privately.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • Players may be replaced at the moderators' discretion for procedural difficulties.
  • All other rules are standard.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on the fdas's Starcraft Brood War Mafia Semi-open setup.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • All night actions happen simultaneously, except roleblock effects which happen first. Roleblocks are resolved with Reasonable Action Resolution, and after all unstopped roleblocks are executed, conflicting roleblocks all apply simultaneously. i.e. if Player X and Y both use roleblock abilities on each other, both effects will be applied.
  • This game is moderated by Mathletesv and fdas. Yes, fdas is the comod even though we usually just call them Mod 1 and 2 in OCRSTM.
  • This game operates using private chats hosted on private forums on AoPS.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contacts us through your role PM or through Discord (Mathletesv#6308) (fdas#8602).
  • For a list of basic resources, refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For a more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, go here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate; access to spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as appropriate.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    • Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • At the end of the day the player with the most votes will be hanged. If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 72 hours (3 days), and each night phase will be 24 hours (1 day).
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1).
    • If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 24 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators.
    • Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves.
    • Do not spam.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
      Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, click here.

  • - Moderation Policies -
  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • Mathletesv and fdas are the moderators of Mafia Game 252.
    • OTHER CONTRIBUTORS/UNPAID INTERNS have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback. fdas wrote flavor.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Purple text is used for third-party flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 (#2be368) and Activity Check blue (#005dff). For a full list of custom colors, click here.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG252 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) 10.
    Current version: 10.3.1
    Version History

    OCRSTM 10 is available for public use. If you are interested in using OCRSTM in your own games, click here.
Setup
This game uses fdas's Starcraft Mafia Mechanics. Please read the following tab.

Mechanics
Overview
Every player will be a unit from Starcraft Brood War. Terran = town Zerg = mafia. Each player will have the same stats as the unit from Starcraft Brood War as of 1.18. All units are considered to be ground units for this game. You can see the stats at https://liquipedia.net/starcraft/Units or on http://classic.battle.net/scc/ . A spreadsheet of all units can be seen here. You can message me at any time to ask for the stats of any unit or if you have questions about anything. Each night a player can attack and deal damage to another player who may be able to attack you back if his range is greater than or equal to yours. Certain units may also have abilities which cost energy that they can use.


Taking damage
Every day, the person who receives a majority of the votes will receive 100 damage (ignores armor). When a person’s total health reaches 0, the person will be dead and out of the game.
During the night, players can choose to attack another player. They will attack that person once. If the other player has equal range to your or greater range then you then they can attack back.
liquipedia wrote:
Damage in StarCraft is applied using basic mathematical equations. It begins with whether the attack hit or missed, if it does hit, the engine calculates the damage as follows: how much damage the attacking unit does, subtracts the armor of the receiving unit, then multiplies by the size vs. damage type modifier. The only exception to the final rule is Shields. Shields instead take full damage from all sources, regardless of the unit's size or damage type modifiers but damage is still reduced by shield armor.
In essence, damage = (attack-armor)*multiplier. Damage will be rounded up.

Units that do normal damage will deal 100% damage to all units.
Units that do concussive damage will deal 100% damage to small units, 50% damage to medium units, and 25% damage to large units.
Units that do explosive damage will deal 50% damage to small units, 75% damage to medium units, and 100% damage to large units.

Some abilities only work on mechanical/biological units
The following are mechanical units: Science Vessel, Battlecruiser, SCV, Vulture, Goliath
All other units are biological, and the SCV counts as both biological and mechanical.

Quote:
Health/Energy Regeneration

If you are a zerg unit then at the end of each night you will regain 10 health. Your current health cannot exceed your maximum health.
Units regain 20 energy at the end of each night.
People will not be able to see exactly how much health/shields they currently have, but they can see the color of their health bar. Green is between 6/7 and 7/7, yellow is between 4/7 and 6/7, orange is between 2/7 and 4/7, red is between 0 and 2/7.
People can see how much energy they currently have.
In your role pm, name a unit in Starcraft 2 but not in in Starcraft 1.


This game runs a semi-open version of the setups that were run in MG179 and MG145.

The Setup
13 players
1 ultralisk
1 defiler
1 queen
10 Terran, to be determined randomly

Method of determining town roles.
Each role is assigned a weight in points and a probability of being chosen.
Points
Science Vessel: 6 points 5%
Battlecruiser: 5 points 5%
Medic: 4 points 10%
SCV: 4 points 10%
Vulture: 3 points 5%
Goliath: 2 points 55%
Ghost: 2 points 10%


Town is given 18 points. For each player, their role is randomly assigned and the points are deducted from the total. If there are not enough points left then the role can no longer be given. Once the number of points runs out everyone else is made a marine. Only one science vessel is allowed.

[*]First-timers can receive up to [b]3 warnings[/b] before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. [b]All other players can receive up to 2.[/b]
[*]Players may be replaced at the moderators' discretion for procedural difficulties. 
[*][b]All other rules are standard.[/b][/list] 

[b]Setup and Moderators[/b]

[list][*]This game is based on the [b]fdas's Starcraft Brood War Mafia Semi-open setup[/b].
[*]Mafia have access to [b]daychat[/b]. 
[*]All night actions happen simultaneously, except roleblock effects which happen first. Roleblocks are resolved with Reasonable Action Resolution, and after all unstopped roleblocks are executed, conflicting roleblocks all apply simultaneously. i.e. if Player X and Y both use roleblock abilities on each other, both effects will be applied.
[*]This game is moderated by Mathletesv and fdas. Yes, fdas is the comod even though we usually just call them Mod 1 and 2 in OCRSTM.  
[*]This game operates using private chats hosted on private forums on AoPS.[/list]

[b]Resources and Support[/b]

[list][*]For any issues, you may contacts us through your role PM or through Discord (Mathletesv#6308) (fdas#8602). 
[*]For a list of basic resources, refer to the Resources tab below. 
[*]For a more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, go [url=aops.com/community/h2517106]here[/url].
[*]If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer.[/list][/hide]

[list][*][hide=General]
[i]Credits to [url=http://bit.ly/fbith]Binomial-theorem[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmchao]chaotic_iak[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmww123]willwang123[/url], and [url=http://bit.ly/ffdas]fdas[/url], among others, as well as the [url=http://bit.ly/MUnFR]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url], for the rule template.[/i]

[b]The following are standard mafia game rules.[/b]


[list][*]Do not post during the night phase.
[*]Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
[*]If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate; access to spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered. 
[*]Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [verbatim][color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent][/verbatim]. 
[*]Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as appropriate. 
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*]Do not impersonate the mods. 
[*]Do not lie to the mods. 
[*]Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement. 
[*]You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[list]
[*] Post votes in code tags in the format [code][code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code][/code]
Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like [code]Vote: fdas[/code]
[*] Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
[*] Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
[*] You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post [code]Vote: No Hang[/code]
[*] You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format [code]Vote: Unvote[/code]
An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
[*] Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
[*] If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
[*] If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
[*] At the end of the day the player with the most votes will be hanged. If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*] The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
[*] During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
[*] Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 72 hours (3 days), and each night phase will be 24 hours (1 day). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1). 
[*]If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance.  
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 24 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM. 
[*]Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.[/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617334]click here[/url]. 


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves. 
[*]Do not spam.
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
[*]Be mature and use common sense.
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
[b]Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617342]click here[/url].[/hide]

[hide=Setup]

[b]This game uses fdas's Starcraft Mafia Mechanics. Please read the following tab.[/b]

[hide=Mechanics]
[b]Overview[/b]
Every player will be a unit from Starcraft Brood War. Terran = town Zerg = mafia. Each player will have the same stats as the unit from Starcraft Brood War as of 1.18. All units are considered to be ground units for this game. You can see the stats at https://liquipedia.net/starcraft/Units or on http://classic.battle.net/scc/ . A spreadsheet of all units can be seen here. You can message me at any time to ask for the stats of any unit or if you have questions about anything. Each night a player can attack and deal damage to another player who may be able to attack you back if his range is greater than or equal to yours. Certain units may also have abilities which cost energy that they can use.


[b]Taking damage[/b]
Every day, the person who receives a majority of the votes will receive 100 damage (ignores armor). When a person’s total health reaches 0, the person will be dead and out of the game.
During the night, players can choose to attack another player. They will attack that person once. If the other player has equal range to your or greater range then you then they can attack back.
[quote=liquipedia]Damage in StarCraft is applied using basic mathematical equations. It begins with whether the attack hit or missed, if it does hit, the engine calculates the damage as follows: how much damage the attacking unit does, subtracts the armor of the receiving unit, then multiplies by the size vs. damage type modifier. The only exception to the final rule is Shields. Shields instead take full damage from all sources, regardless of the unit's size or damage type modifiers but damage is still reduced by shield armor.
[/quote]
In essence, damage = (attack-armor)*multiplier. Damage will be rounded up.

Units that do normal damage will deal 100% damage to all units.
Units that do concussive damage will deal 100% damage to small units, 50% damage to medium units, and 25% damage to large units.
Units that do explosive damage will deal 50% damage to small units, 75% damage to medium units, and 100% damage to large units.

Some abilities only work on mechanical/biological units
The following are mechanical units: Science Vessel, Battlecruiser, SCV, Vulture, Goliath
All other units are biological, and the SCV counts as both biological and mechanical.


[quote]Health/Energy Regeneration[/quote]

If you are a zerg unit then at the end of each night you will regain 10 health. Your current health cannot exceed your maximum health.
Units regain 20 energy at the end of each night.
People will not be able to see exactly how much health/shields they currently have, but they can see the color of their health bar. Green is between 6/7 and 7/7, yellow is between 4/7 and 6/7, orange is between 2/7 and 4/7, red is between 0 and 2/7.
People can see how much energy they currently have.
In your role pm, name a unit in Starcraft 2 but not in in Starcraft 1.[/hide]

[b]This game runs a semi-open version of the setups that were run in [url=https://aops.com/community/p11545575]MG179[/url] and [url=https://aops.com/community/p8564343]MG145[/url].[/b]

[hide="The Setup"]13 players
[color=#f00]1 ultralisk
1 defiler
1 queen[/color]
[color=#0f0]10 Terran, to be determined randomly[/color]

Method of determining town roles.
Each role is assigned a weight in points and a probability of being chosen.
[hide=Points]Science Vessel: 6 points 5%
Battlecruiser: 5 points 5%
Medic: 4 points 10%
SCV: 4 points 10%
Vulture: 3 points 5%
Goliath: 2 points 55%
Ghost: 2 points 10%
[/hide]

Town is given 18 points. For each player, their role is randomly assigned and the points are deducted from the total. If there are not enough points left then the role can no longer be given. Once the number of points runs out everyone else is made a marine. Only one science vessel is allowed.[/hide]
